title: Chapter 346 – Wu and Yue in the Same Boat (2)
content:
It was a chaotic era.

Reliable information was exceedingly scarce. For any given incident, a dozen rumors might circulate, yet the truth could typically be found in only one or two.

Even the portraits of the current Radiant Demon Wing Leader that circulated mainly through the central provinces reflected this reality.

The likenesses varied tremendously from one painting to the next, though their total number remained modest compared to the breadth of his fame.

This was because sharing private information about high-ranking officials of Desolate Fortress could invite punishment from government offices.

'This feeling.'

Jeong Yeon-shin had studied twelve different facial renderings, each with its own style and accompanying description. He could not dismiss the documents personally entrusted to him by Cheong Myeong, Baek Mi-ryeo, Yue Shou-lin, and the Grand Administrator before his departure.

Their thoughtfulness had broadened his knowledge considerably. He had absorbed a wealth of detailed anecdotes about the figure he was now about to face.

The person before him matched several of those descriptions unmistakably.

"How strange. I heard you were roughly three years younger than me... yet your body is fully grown."

The figure still loosely gripping a fishing rod glanced over at Jeong Yeon-shin as he spoke.

Crisp enunciation delivered in a rough voice. As though an invisible rake were scraping a throat that should have produced deep, resonant tones.

His outward appearance was equally arresting. It carried the bearing befitting the legitimate bloodline of Ming Cult, said to have deceived heaven for generations.

It began with the silver mask that descended only from forehead to nose tip, leaving the lower face elegantly exposed.

Despite bearing no special engravings or patterns, a faint luster rippled across its surface like gentle waves. It complemented the crooked smile that seemed permanently etched on his lips.

He was draped in an extravagantly splendid white windproof robe, its edges traced with black embroidery thread that served as decoration without tipping into excess.

This restraint owed itself to the overwhelming presence emanating from the dark blue eyes behind the mask. Even the neck wrap fashioned from pure white animal fur looked less like a garment and more like the mane of some divine dragon.

"Your curse doesn't sound like a curse."

The strange figure moved his lips slowly.

In the silences between syllables, the winter stream's characteristic cool stillness settled around them with particular weight.

Whether he was actually fishing or not seemed incidental. A truly overwhelming presence saturated the surroundings.

"Perhaps it's your uncommon appearance, but the word 'Damn it' sounds rather amusing coming from you. It must be that gentle nature of yours."

"......"

Jeong Yeon-shin thought in silence: I should be wary of this person's discernment.

Invincible Yelü Zhen.

The world-renowned greatest genius of the vast world.

A figure who had blocked Jeong Yeon-shin's path with remarkably startling intelligence.

The clothing concealed how well-trained the body underneath might be, and the mask—more luxury ornament than anything functional—covered half his face. This meant gauging any information about his martial arts was all but impossible without observing large movements and energy waves.

Woman or man?

How deeply trained was he in physical martial arts beyond spell techniques?

Nothing could be determined with certainty.

The windproof robe encasing his body appeared to be something close to a dharma treasure. It blocked energy waves so thoroughly that nothing was detectable.

'I shouldn't exchange too many moves.'

A feeling unlike any he had known before crept through him. It was closer to intuition edging toward insight.

Jeong Yeon-shin engraved the image of a dragon holding a fan in its mouth within his mind.

So that he could overlay the mental image onto the Flowing Star Sword at his waist and launch the Falling Star Sword at the opponent's neck at any moment. Among all his martial arts, none possessed a faster draw than the Fan Dragon Treading Lotus Technique.

'Alternatively, I should consider Ultimate Thunder. My head might leave my shoulders before the Falling Star Sword can fly.'

He opened his mouth.

"Ming Cult's Yelü Zhen. Have you clashed with Desolate Fortress martial artists before?"

The instant the question left his lips in a monotone voice, energy waves erupted like wildfire from various points throughout the skeletal forest, pressing down on Jeong Yeon-shin.

Dozens of them.

Snow on the branches shattered in every direction, turning the world white. The density of each energy wave was uniformly immense.

Among them, the internal energy release speed of the first to act could not be explained without Three Flowers Gathering at the Crown. This confirmed that the Ming Cult's Young Sect Leader commanded sword bearers comparable to Desolate Fortress's Black-ranks.

It was then.

Whoosh!

The sparse, desolate snowflakes drifting down were shoved aside by a large circular shockwave.

Its source was beside Jeong Yeon-shin. The Seventh Apostle, who had revealed one ominously bewitching ruby-like eye, was surveying their surroundings with measured care. Her back perfectly straight.

Reddish energy currents that had radiated from her entire body were seeping into the snow like scattered flower petals.

Presently, words that might well have been uttered by Invincible's own subordinates flowed from the Seventh Apostle's lips.

"How dare you."

Hiya—

The Ming Cult's Young Sect Leader let out a low exclamation.

"Your Wife is quite extraordinary. Ordering a joint attack would prove costly."

"Answer me."

Jeong Yeon-shin pressed for a response, his expression indifferent.

There was a startled rustling of sleeves being gently adjusted beside him, but his priority at this moment was learning the whereabouts of his seniors.

"Radiant Demon Wing Leader... indeed, you have spirit. You deserve to wear such a large 'Desolate' character in broad daylight. These days, among the more capable martial artists, Desolate Fortress people are known as targets to be plundered. Especially so in Sichuan of late."

The Ming Cult's Young Sect Leader rose to his feet.

Invincible tossed aside the bamboo fishing rod with casual indifference, letting it sprawl on the ground while muttering some complaint to himself through the mouth visible beneath the mask.

"They say fishing is about catching time, but truly, not a single fish was caught. How frustrating."

"Targets to be plundered?"

Jeong Yeon-shin asked. The Young Sect Leader, who glanced at him, nodded with idle ease.

"You might not have heard this yet in Huguang. Well... that's how things stand. Desolate Fortress martial artists, I mean. They carry ample supplies and precious martial arts knowledge in their heads, yet there are no consequences for targeting them. You're the busiest people in these chaotic times."

He explained that Desolate Fortress routinely neglected the safety of its martial artists, sending them on solo missions to tend to the common people.

"Of course, you're still harbingers of death to third-rate sects, but powerful masters who had avoided White or Blue ranks only out of fear of Desolate Fortress's retaliation... rather than starving to death during a famine while obeying the law, isn't it preferable to strike first? Whether that means plundering villages or killing the Desolate Fortress martial artists who come to stop them."

Desolate Fortress martial artists whose whereabouts had gone dark throughout the world... It was not limited to Obeying Heaven Squad, Forest-Treading Squad, and Immortal Tree Spirit Squad. What had once been uncommon was gradually becoming routine. Many lower-ranked martial artists were failing to return.

Jeong Yeon-shin had been hearing about such incidents since his initiation.

On Sichuan's City of Renowned Craftsmen, they had lost many Radiant Demon Wing seniors, and even after he became the Radiant Demon Wing Leader, reports of Desolate Fortress martial artists dying far from home never ceased. Such stories were a constant companion.

"People are bound to be swept along by the tide of the times. Would Desolate Fortress be any exception?"

A flash of light gleamed in the Young Sect Leader's dark blue eyes. It seemed to pierce straight through Jeong Yeon-shin's innermost thoughts.

"The answer you want—well, it may not amount to much. There are so many peerless masters wandering the world that counting them on two hands falls short, and your companions may have encountered one of them and been defeated somewhere."

"Not you?"

"Well. I am certainly one of those peerless masters, but... you don't believe the rumors spread by the Yu Ling, do you?"

Those people are experts at driving wedges between the martial world and Desolate Fortress. The Young Sect Leader, who had leisurely interlocked his fingers, glanced at Jeong Yeon-shin and spoke again.

"You don't seem to put much stock in it. Quite right—martial artists must first exchange blows. I really do hate pain, though..."

In that moment, the silence of the snow field seemed to take on a tangible weight. Jeong Yeon-shin felt an invisible pressure bearing down on his shoulders.

None of the Ming Cult masters who had reacted so intensely to his earlier words revealed themselves. This despite the situation hovering on the brink of conflict.

The meaning was plain. They held absolute confidence in the "Invincible" title attached to their master's alias. Just as the trust that Radiant Demon Wing masters placed in Jeong Yeon-shin.

"Shall we exchange two or three moves? Your energy is deeply unsettling, but a master's honest intentions are revealed through their techniques."

Black flames flickered beneath the Young Sect Leader's mask. There was no sign of martial arts activation, no crackling sound.

A formidable power radiated from the black flames that wrapped around and climbed the thick windproof robe.

It was a natural progression. From the very beginning, this was a relationship in which trust held no place.

Jeong Yeon-shin released the internal energy containment method he had inherited from the Blood Guardian Leader. Simultaneously, a white lotus bloomed beneath his feet.

Luminous Wheel Energy, Brilliant River—Ultimate Thunder. Full power from the opening move. The first time since his bone transformation. This opponent warranted nothing less.

***

https://discord.gg/XC9DTsTQ9Z

***

Devastation surrounded them. Amid smoke that raged as if borne by a typhoon, corpses lay everywhere.

This was a place where the great matters of the martial world were being decided. It could be called the highest echelon of the martial world.

"Desolate Fortress will be exterminated."

A calm, measured voice. A man swathed entirely in black robes. The lower face with its dark beard, visible beneath the bamboo hat crowning his head, moved slowly.

"The net of heaven and earth woven by all the major factions of the world will see to it. Perhaps only the Nine Great Sects will be excluded."

"Talk sense."

Ten paces away.

A man with a scholarly bearing replied. He wore a purple robe that closely resembled a scholar's attire.

Despite being encircled by enemies who glared at him with undisguised hostility, not a single one dared approach the unarmed man.

This held true even though only one young man in blue martial attire stood as his ally, brow furrowed.

The black-robed man in the bamboo hat—the Yu Ling Leader—faced them squarely. With an attitude of extreme leisure.

"You shouldn't have trusted the Eight Great Clans. Even after suffering as you did... I know your chivalrous spirit, which makes it all the more regrettable, Yong. The Divine Sword Squad members who will return as corpses from Anping Mountain over there won't be able to close their eyes for the resentment."

Behind the Divine Sword Group Leader stood a middle-aged swordsman with long blue sleeves hanging at his sides, blocking the escape route.

The overwhelming presence radiating from his snow-white sword filled the world like a semi-transparent mountain, forbidding all movement. It was an imperial sword form.

"Group Leader. Please release the martial world now."

Namgung First Sword of the Martial Dragon Association said.

They were not alone. They knew that the Dark Night Palace Leader's death-dealing arrows were trained on them from twenty li beyond where the Divine Sword Group Leader and the Divine Sword Squad were burning.

The Yu Ling Leader said:

"The net of heaven and earth designed to defeat the seventeen squads of the Divine Sword Group one by one is complete. Among them, the annihilation of your Divine Sword Squad will mark the beginning of the grand plan. The same holds true for the squad leaders dispatched westward from Desolate Fortress. Xinjiang, Qinghai, Sichuan. There are more than five of them."

He spoke as if mentioning lives already forfeit. The master of Yu Ling. If one were to name the foremost messenger of death in the martial world, he would be counted first without hesitation. He was an absolute being.

The corner of the Divine Sword Group Leader's mouth rose.

He had weathered such situations many times before, and had endured for a very long time the sight of unaffiliated martial artists swaggering about like ruffians who had obtained divine power.

Since inheriting the supreme hierarchy from Tyrant Hero Ma Yeon-jeok, he had lived like a wall silently holding back the fierce winds.

"These fellows are getting on my nerves."

He muttered.

Swoosh.

Suddenly, writing implements rose from the travel pack sprawled at the Divine Sword Group Leader's feet. The marvel of void absorption. Brush, ink, inkstone, and seventeen sheets of paper danced through the air of their own accord.

"What trick is this?"

The enemies dared not move rashly. The Divine Sword Group Leader had not yet drawn his Coiling Dragon Sword—his life-governing technique.

This meant that though they had poisoned him by heavenly fortune, they had not diminished his full power. They needed to buy time.

"Group Leader?"

The Divine Sword Squad's Blue-rank martial artist, Yeon So-ha, looked up at his superior. The Divine Sword Group Leader burst into laughter.

"I don't think I can remain a hero after all. Check the contents. My energy sense has grown dull. If the later pages aren't fully written, copy them exactly as they are."

Yeon So-ha faithfully obeyed the Group Leader's order. The youthful man turned his back to the enemies and examined the contents of the letter.

Then his shoulders heaved once, greatly.

"Really?"

He asked.

"Should I really write it like this? All seventeen pages? When you gathered the squad leaders' lots, was this what you had in mind...?"

"It seems you're indirectly asking whether I've lost my mind."

"No, really?"

Yeon So-ha was a loyal subordinate. Taking the unfinished page from the void absorption, he picked up a brush and began copying while posing the question once more.

The Divine Sword Group Leader smirked.

"The stubborn old men of the Elder Council will unanimously try to strip this robe from me for once. But having overheard their plans, what choice do I have?"

"Would it end at just that? What about the common people?"

"...It's a sin I must bear. In my judgment as Group Leader, this is better than all of us being destroyed and exterminated together."

His voice was very heavy.

But that was all. The Divine Sword Group Leader did not revoke his instructions.

Rustle!

He simply spread his hands wide, folding the letters suspended in the void absorption and sending them soaring in every direction.

Those documents were tied directly to the legs of one hawk and more than ten swallows circling in the sky above.

A martial skill reaching the realm of the divine. The sword winds and hidden weapons launched by the enemies encircling them could not draw near—they were torn apart before they arrived.

This was because the Divine Sword Group Leader had already drawn a rainbow-colored radiant sword, pouring forth tremendous sword energy.

"Go."

Until the seventeen spirit beasts of Desolate Fortress beat their wings and scattered in all directions.

The Yu Ling Leader and Namgung First Sword did not stop them. This was because they had assessed the situation with their highly developed upper dantians.

The moment they attempted to interfere with those letters, the Divine Sword Group Leader would disregard his own life and force a mutual end.

"Curious about what I wrote?"

The Divine Sword Group Leader asked. The Yu Ling Leader slowly nodded.

"I can guess. But can you bear the consequences? The imperial court won't overlook what you've done."

"Does it send chills down your spine? Martial world scum like you thought our fortress would simply continue to absorb the blows."

No answer came.

The Divine Sword Group Leader picked up the one remaining letter with his fingertip. At the same moment, smoke from Samadhi True Fire curled up from his fingers.

The burning letter. Words that would shake the world were written in uneven handwriting, gradually consumed from the corners inward.

[Personal letter to Squad Leaders. To the esteemed ones.

I'm terribly sorry during this busy time. I know everyone is in a state of emergency. Therefore, I don't know how many will open this letter, but praying to heaven and getting straight to the point...

The Divine Sword Group Leader commands.

Temporarily ignore any harm caused by martial ruffians. The Divine Sword Group of Desolate Fortress will now secure our own safety from the entire martial world. We will show them what happens when the main fortress unites as they have.

Black-ranks under my command are to cease all missions immediately upon seeing these words and gather at Sichuan's Ten Perfections Sect.

Seventeen.

All of you.]
